summary: Deleted two private GX unknown headers that were causing `ctx.c` compiler errors. The removed files only contained provisional declarations for unsplit/private GX symbols, including GX texture/framebuffer float constants and prototypes for `GXLoadTexObjPreLoaded`, `GXInitTlutRegion`, and `GXInitTexCacheRegion`. There was no PR body or review discussion, so the exact compiler failure mode is not documented in the slice.
what_changed: Removed `src/dolphin/gx/GX_unknown_001/__GX_unknown_001.h`, a private header described in-file as shared private declarations for unsplit GX files.
Removed extern declarations for several `GXTexture_804DE...` and `GXFrameBuf_804DE...` float constants from the deleted nested GX unknown header.
Removed the provisional prototype `GXLoadTexObjPreLoaded(unk_t, unk_t, s32)` from the deleted nested GX unknown header.
Removed `src/dolphin/gx/__GX_unknown_001.h`, a private GX-module declaration header.
Removed provisional prototypes for `GXInitTlutRegion(s32*, s32, s32)` and `GXInitTexCacheRegion(unk_t, s8, u32, s32, u32, s32, s32)` from the deleted top-level GX unknown header.
smart_moves: Fixed the compiler issue by deleting unused or problematic private unknown headers instead of adding more workaround declarations.
Avoided preserving provisional `unk_t`-heavy prototypes when they were not safe for the current build context.
Removed duplicate-looking private header layers under both `src/dolphin/gx/` and `src/dolphin/gx/GX_unknown_001/`, reducing ambiguity for include resolution and generated context compilation.
decomp_lessons: Private scratch headers for unsplit files can break global context-compilation workflows such as `ctx.c` if they expose incomplete, stale, or conflicting declarations.
When temporary headers contain only provisional declarations and are not required by compiled translation units, deletion can be safer than trying to paper over compiler errors.
Headers with names like `__GX_unknown_001.h` should be treated as temporary scaffolding; once they interfere with build tooling, move declarations to real split-section headers or remove them.
Keep unknown GX declarations close to the source file or split module that actually needs them rather than maintaining broad shared private headers.
naming_conventions: The removed headers used double-underscore private names: `__GX_unknown_001.h`.
The deleted include guards mirrored paths in all caps: `DOLPHIN_GX_GX_UNKNOWN_001___GX_UNKNOWN_001_H` and `DOLPHIN_GX___GX_UNKNOWN_001_H`.
Unknown data symbols used address-suffixed names such as `GXTexture_804DE28C` and `GXFrameBuf_804DE228`.
The header comments identified these as shared/private declarations for the GX module and unsplit GX files, not public Dolphin GX API headers.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No direct assembly matching tactic is documented in the PR slice.
The fix supports build/context generation hygiene by removing provisional declarations that could interfere with compiler parsing.
The deleted declarations included address comments such as `/* 004DAE6C */`, preserving evidence that these were decomp-address placeholder symbols rather than finalized API names.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the PR slice.
No reviewer rationale was captured; the only explicit rationale is the PR title, `Fix ctx.c compiler errors`.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that reintroduced or relocated `GXLoadTexObjPreLoaded`, `GXInitTlutRegion`, or `GXInitTexCacheRegion` declarations.
Search for remaining includes of `src/dolphin/gx/__GX_unknown_001.h` or `src/dolphin/gx/GX_unknown_001/__GX_unknown_001.h` after PR 1246.
Investigate the exact `ctx.c` compiler errors from the build logs if available outside this PR slice.
Check whether the deleted `GXTexture_804DE...` and `GXFrameBuf_804DE...` constants were later renamed or moved into split GX source files.
